skill.name: file-organizer
skill.description: Intelligently organizes files and folders by understanding context, finding duplicates, and suggesting better organizational structures. Use when user wants to clean up directories, organize downloads, remove duplicates, or restructure projects.
skill.summary: Analyze cluttered folders, identify duplicates, and plan safer file organization with user approval.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for external commands are true positives because the skill instructs shell-based directory inspection and file moves. The high weak-crypto and reconnaissance findings are false positives except for one md5 duplicate-check command, which is not used for security but should not be the sole basis for deletion. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: User-Scoped Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides shell command examples using ls, find, file, du, sort, uniq, mkdir, and mv. These are legitimate for file organization, but they can reveal local file metadata or alter files if used on overly broad paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in fenced examples and match the scanner findings. Context shows a legitimate file-management workflow, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Potentially Destructive File Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill says it can move, rename, organize, and delete duplicate or trash files after approval. It includes confirmation requirements, but the workflow still carries risk if users approve broad changes without backups.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly describes moving, renaming, and possible deletion. The built-in approval instructions reduce severity but do not remove the risk of accidental data loss.

medium_findings.title: Non-Cryptographic Hash Used for Duplicate Detection
medium_findings.description: The md5 command is used to find duplicate files, not to protect secrets or verify trust. This makes the weak-cryptography blocker mostly a false positive, but duplicate removal should compare size and paths before deletion.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The md5 usage is directly visible and the duplicate-detection context is clear. Risk is medium because hash collisions are unlikely in routine cleanup but the command could influence deletion decisions.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Prose
low_findings.description: Several high and low static findings point to ordinary description, folder naming, or file naming guidance rather than weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, or network reconnaissance. No evidence found of network access or stealth system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible prose or examples about organizing downloads and naming files. They do not contain network calls, credential access, or security-sensitive cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Directory Enumeration Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends commands that list directories, inspect file metadata, and summarize file sizes. These commands are expected for the skill but should be limited to user-approved directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell snippets are explicit and actionable. The risk depends on scope, because listing broad directories can expose sensitive file names and metadata.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Move and Rename Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes mkdir and mv examples for reorganizing content. These operations are normal for the skill but can cause data loss or workflow disruption if applied incorrectly.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and target filesystem changes. The surrounding instructions require approval and logging, which lowers but does not eliminate risk.

content.user_title: Organize Files with Safer Cleanup Plans
content.value_statement: Messy folders make files hard to find and duplicates hard to trust. This skill reviews a target directory, proposes a structure, and guides approved cleanup steps.
actual_capabilities: Reviews a user-selected directory and summarizes its structure.
Identifies file types, sizes, date ranges, and duplicate candidates.
Suggests logical folders by type, purpose, project, or date.
Creates an organization plan before making file changes.
Guides folder creation, file moves, and renames after approval.
Provides a post-cleanup summary and maintenance tips.
limitations: Requires access to local files and explicit user-approved paths.
Shell command examples may need changes for different operating systems.
Cannot fully know whether a file is important from metadata alone.
Duplicate detection should be verified before any file is deleted.

use_cases.title: Clean a Downloads Folder
use_cases.description: Sort downloads into documents, images, archives, installers, and files that need review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Project Archives
use_cases.description: Review old project folders, separate active work from archives, and document the final structure.

use_cases.title: Reduce Duplicate Storage
use_cases.description: Find likely duplicate files, compare names and sizes, and decide what should be kept.

prompt_templates.title: Organize One Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner cleanup of a single directory.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me organize this folder: [path]. First summarize what is inside, then propose a plan before changing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Find Duplicate Candidates
prompt_templates.scenario: Review possible duplicate files without deleting them.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan [path] for duplicate candidates. Show the paths, sizes, dates, and a recommendation for which copy to keep.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Archive Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Separate active files from old material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review [path] and propose an archive structure for files that appear inactive or completed. Do not move files until I approve.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Project Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a mixed work directory.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze [path] and design a project-based folder structure. Include conflict handling, rollback notes, and files that need my decision.

output: A summary of file counts, common file types, and largest items.
A proposed folder structure for documents, images, archives, installers, and review items.
A confirmation request before creating folders or moving files.

output_examples.input: Find duplicates in this archive folder.
output: A grouped list of duplicate candidates with paths, sizes, and dates.
A recommendation for the best copy to keep.
A warning to verify matches before deleting anything.

output_examples.input: Help me clean up an old project directory.
output: A current-state summary with active, archive, and uncertain folders.
A list of files that need a human decision.

best_practices: Start with one approved directory instead of an entire home folder.
Back up important data before approving moves, renames, or deletion.
Review duplicate candidates manually before removing any file.
anti_patterns: Do not run cleanup commands on broad system paths without review.
Do not delete files based only on matching names or hashes.
Do not reorganize active project folders without checking tool expectations.

faq.question: Can this skill delete files?
faq.answer: It can recommend deleting duplicates or trash files, but it instructs the assistant to ask for confirmation first.

faq.question: Does it need access to my files?
faq.answer: Yes. It works by inspecting a user-selected local directory and should stay within that approved scope.

faq.question: Can it organize by project instead of file type?
faq.answer: Yes. It can group files by project, purpose, date, or file type based on the directory contents.

faq.question: Will it work on every operating system?
faq.answer: Some command examples are platform-specific and may need adjustment before use.

faq.question: How does it handle duplicate files?
faq.answer: It identifies duplicate candidates and asks the user to confirm which files should be kept or removed.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive folders?
faq.answer: Use caution. Limit the scope, avoid exposing sensitive paths in shared chats, and review every proposed change.
